vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by the white rabbit anchor and soil debris. The environment is currently a collection of empty or dormant pots.

inventory_reconciliation.p1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.p2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.p3: Systemic Loss: Pothos absent; only white rabbit anchor remains.
inventory_reconciliation.p4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in shared black pot.

plant_audit.p1: Empty soil surface; no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.p2: Empty soil surface; no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.p3: White rabbit anchor present; no Pothos foliage detected.
plant_audit.p4: Empty soil surface; no signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Soil appears dark and potentially over-saturated or compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of a spoon in the early images suggests human intervention; white rabbit anchor is the only consistent object.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows a spoon and pots; no clear plant life.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_current: The environment remains static; no growth or recovery observed. The white rabbit anchor remains the only stable element.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: Total systemic failure. The biome is currently devoid of viable plant life. The lack of chlorophyll-bearing tissue suggests either total senescence or complete removal of the specimens.
anomalies: Spoon in early images (removed in later frames).
White rabbit anchor is the only constant.
Persistent absence of expected botanical specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete absence of the expected botanical registry. The pots are present but appear to contain only soil, with no visible plant growth. The white rabbit anchor in P3 is the only non-soil element remaining. The environment appears to be in a state of total botanical loss, with no signs of recovery or new growth over the observed 5-day period.
